title: New Futures Mobility Fund 2026: Comprehensive Guide for Applicants in Australia
summary: The New Futures Mobility Fund , delivered by the Australian Academy of Science , is a major national initiative designed to empower humanitarian visa holders living in Australia by supporting their participation in recognised scientific conferences, symposia, workshops, and other STEM‑focused professional events across the country. This program removes financial barriers, strengthens research visibility, and helps emerging and established researchers build meaningful professional networks within Australia’s scientific community. Overview of the New Futures Mobility Fund The New Futures Mobility Fund provides competitive mobility grants to eligible applicants who wish to attend structured scientific or professional development events in Australia. These grants are intended to: Strengthen research capability Expand professional networks Enhance career development Increase visibility within Australia’s STEM community Remove financial barriers that prevent participation in scientific events The program is made possible through a generous gift from Jaishree Srinivasan and Academy Fellow Emeritus Professor Srini Srinivasan AM FAA FRS . Key Dates for the 2026 Application Cycle Applicants must take note of the following timeline: 18 May 2026 – Applications open 09 July 2026 – Applications close (deadline) September 2026 – Notification of outcome October 2026 – Funding agreements issued and funded activities may commence Program Highlights The New Futures Mobility Fund offers a range of benefits designed to support professional growth: Funding support to attend recognised scientific conferences and similar STEM events in Australia Career development opportunities through networking and visibility Flexible funding to reduce cost barriers and increase accessibility Support for research training , research activity, and professional development Opportunities to engage with Australia’s scientific community and expand research networks 1. Eligibility Requirements Applicant Eligibility To apply, individuals must meet all the following criteria at the time of application: Be living in Australia Hold an eligible humanitarian visa subclass Be engaged in research training or research activity in Australia Be seeking funding to attend a recognised STEM scientific conference, symposium, workshop, or similar event within Australia Eligible Humanitarian Visa Subclasses Refugee (subclass 200) In-country Special Humanitarian (subclass 201) Global Special Humanitarian visa (subclass 202)** Emergency Rescue (subclass 203) Woman at Risk (subclass 204) Protection visa (subclass 866)** Note: As of 29 May 2026, subclasses 866 and 202 have been newly added as eligible visa types. Eligible Applicant Profiles Masters students PhD candidates Early- and mid-career researchers (EMCRs) with a Masters or PhD awarded up to 15 years prior to the application deadline Extensions may be considered for significant career interruptions 2. Eligible Activities Activities must be Australia-based and must clearly demonstrate how participation will strengthen the applicant’s research capability, networks, and career development. Eligible Activities Include: Recognised scientific conferences, symposia, and workshops Specialist training courses or masterclasses linked to scientific events Professional development opportunities supporting research training or activity Research translation or industry engagement forums with a STEM focus Other professional opportunities approved by the Academy The Academy may request clarification if the scientific relevance of an event is unclear. 3. Fields of Research Applications are welcome from all STEM fields , including: Science Technology Engineering Mathematics 4. Eligible Australian Research Organisations (EROs) Applicants must be employed by or affiliated with an eligible research organisation, such as: Australian tertiary education institutions Cooperative research centres Publicly funded research agencies State or territory-funded research organisations Public or private Australian research companies Australian not-for-profit research organisations Other incorporated Australian entities Note: EROs must be registered for GST Universities must be income tax exempt Non-corporate Commonwealth entities are not eligible 5. Additional Program Information Terms of Award Covers funding conditions, allowable expenses, and grant management requirements. Application Process Applicants must submit through the Academy’s online portal and provide all required documentation. Assessment Process Applications are evaluated based on eligibility, merit, relevance of the event, and potential impact on the applicant’s research career. Reporting Requirements Successful applicants must submit a final report detailing outcomes and benefits gained from the funded activity. Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more grant opportunities here The post New Futures Mobility Fund 2026: Comprehensive Guide for Applicants in Australia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: GIZ Trainee Programmes: Launch Your Global Development Career with World-Class Training
summary: The Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH offers some of the most respected and competitive trainee programmes in the international development sector. Designed for young professionals seeking hands-on experience, global exposure, and structured professional growth, these programmes prepare future leaders for impactful careers in development cooperation, financial management, and strategic communications. GIZ’s trainee tracks include: German Development Cooperation (DC) Trainee Programme Financial Management Trainee Programme (International Locations) Public Relations (PR) Traineeship Each programme provides a unique pathway into the world of international cooperation, offering structured training, mentorship, and opportunities to work across Germany and GIZ partner countries worldwide. 1. German Development Cooperation (DC) Trainee Programme The DC Trainee Programme is implemented by GIZ on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ). It is specifically designed to cultivate the next generation of development cooperation professionals. What the DC Trainee Programme Offers Participants gain deep insight into: Coordination and management processes in development cooperation Policy frameworks and implementation strategies Project cycle management International development structures and partnerships Technical and managerial responsibilities within development organizations Upon completion, trainees are equipped to take on demanding roles in German and international development institutions. Key Features Structured rotations across GIZ departments Exposure to global development challenges Professional mentorship Preparation for technical and leadership roles 2. Financial Management Trainee Programme (International Locations) This 18‑month programme is ideal for graduates in business administration or related fields who want to build a career in international financial management. Programme Structure The programme includes: Three-week onboarding in Germany Rotations (2–8 weeks each) across key GIZ units , including: Finance Procurement and Contracting International Services Regional Departments Legal Affairs and Insurance Internal Audit and Compliance A 12‑month international assignment , where trainees: Work on a full business administration cycle Manage financial processes in a GIZ project Receive supervision and mentorship Eight weeks of job shadowing at a GIZ country office What Trainees Gain Practical experience in international financial management Exposure to global project operations Hands-on learning in real development contexts Professional growth through structured training 3. Public Relations (PR) Traineeship This 24‑month traineeship is designed for individuals passionate about communications and international cooperation. It prepares trainees for modern PR roles within a global organization. What the PR Traineeship Includes Training within GIZ’s Corporate Communications Unit Writing press releases, online content, and strategic communication materials Organizing internal and external events Managing corporate image and brand consistency Moderating social intranet communities Advising communication teams worldwide Certified Training and External Exposure Participation in accredited PR-sector seminars Completion of a recognized industry qualification Job shadowing at a media company Support from GIZ’s press team in selecting a placement Most training takes place in Bonn and Eschborn, with additional exposure to GIZ’s work in a partner country. Benefits of GIZ Trainee Programmes Flexible working hours International exposure Equal opportunities Extensive training and continuing education Mentorship and professional guidance Strong career prospects within GIZ Application Deadlines GIZ updates application windows throughout the year. Applicants should regularly check the official GIZ career portal for current deadlines and openings. For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more trainee opportunities here The post GIZ Trainee Programmes: Launch Your Global Development Career with World-Class Training appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: The ifa Research Award on International Cultural Relations 2026 — Comprehensive Guide
summary: The ifa Research Award on International Cultural Relations stands as one of the most respected recognitions for emerging scholars whose work advances global understanding of soft power , international cultural relations , and Foreign Cultural and Educational Policy (FCEP) . For over 25 years, the award has celebrated academic excellence, interdisciplinary innovation, and research that strengthens cultural diplomacy worldwide. This detailed article provides a full overview of the 2026 call for applications, eligibility requirements, submission guidelines, benefits, jury composition, and the award’s long-standing legacy. ifa Research Award 2026: A Deep Dive into One of the Leading Global Awards in Cultural Relations The Institut für Auslandsbeziehungen (ifa) has long been a global leader in promoting international cultural dialogue. Through its annual Research Award, ifa supports young scholars whose work contributes meaningfully to the understanding and practice of cultural diplomacy. The award is designed not only to recognize academic excellence but also to amplify the visibility of emerging researchers, support publication of their work, and strengthen the global discourse on cultural relations. Purpose and Vision of the Award The award aims to: Strengthen academic reflection on soft power , international cultural relations , and Foreign Cultural and Educational Policy . Promote interdisciplinary exchange and broaden perspectives in cultural diplomacy. Bring ifa’s international expertise into German and global debates. Support young scholars at different career stages by offering flexible, tailored visibility formats. Encourage research that has practical relevance for cultural policy, institutions, and intercultural practice. Over the past 25 years, 27 scholars have been honored, each contributing to the global understanding of cultural diplomacy. What the Award Includes Award recipients receive a comprehensive support package designed to elevate their research and professional visibility: 1. Public Presentation of Research Awardees can choose a format best suited to their work and career stage, such as: Public lecture Podcast Short video Other innovative formats The goal is to ensure maximum visibility and accessibility of the research. 2. Publication Support ifa covers the printing costs for publishing the award-winning work in its prestigious series: Kultur und Außenpolitik Published by Nomos Verlag Available via open access Subject to publisher review This ensures global reach and academic credibility. Eligibility Criteria Applications must be submitted by a recommending professor on behalf of the young academic. To qualify: Thesis Completion Timeline Must be completed within the last two years No earlier than 30 April 2024 Academic Performance Doctoral theses: at least magna cum laude Master’s theses: at least the second-highest grade Research Focus The thesis must: Examine soft power , international cultural relations , or Foreign Cultural and Educational Policy Demonstrate the significance and effectiveness of these concepts Advance theoretical or practical understanding Offer applicability to cultural policy, institutions, or intercultural practice Be an independent and sound scholarly achievement Recommendation Requirement A formal recommendation from a professor is mandatory. Required Application Documents The recommending professor must submit one single PDF file (max. 15 MB) containing: Complete text of the thesis (PDF) Abstract Letter of recommendation Full assessment or grading documentation (German or English) Declaration of independent work + date of completion/publication Curriculum vitae List of publications (if applicable) Applicant’s contact information (email + institutional affiliation) Language: Submissions may be in German or English . Submission Deadline 30 June 2026 — 11:59 p.m. (CEST) Selection Process and Jury Award winners are chosen by ifa based on recommendations from an independent jury of experts. Jury for 2026 Professor Nicole Colin (Aix-Marseille University) Professor Eveline Dürr (LMU Munich) Professor Julius Heinicke (University of Hildesheim) Legacy of Excellence: Past Award Winners The award has honored outstanding scholars since 2001, including: 2021 – Hendrik W. Ohnesorge 2020 – Tobias Reichard 2019 – Dominik Herzner 2018 – Elisabeth Piller Sonja Großmann 2017 – Lara Jüssen Manuela Sato-Prinz 2016 – Elena Korowin Jens Adam 2015 – Annika Hampel Katayon Meier …and many more distinguished researchers. Additional special recognitions have also been awarded in various years. For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more award opportunities here The post The ifa Research Award on International Cultural Relations 2026 — Comprehensive Guide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Bangladesh Climate & Private Sector Reporting Fellowships 2026: A Complete Guide for Journalists
summary: The Earth Journalism Network (EJN) has announced a major opportunity for journalists in Bangladesh: the 2026 Reporting Fellowships to Produce In‑Depth Stories on Climate Change and the Private Sector . This fellowship aims to strengthen high‑quality, deeply reported journalism that explores how industries, businesses, farmers, and workers in Bangladesh are both affected by—and contributing to—climate change. Application Deadline: June 14, 2026, 11:59 PM (Asia/Dhaka) Accepted Application Languages: English and Bangla This article provides a full breakdown of the fellowship, including its purpose, themes, eligibility, budget requirements, judging criteria, and application process. Overview: Why This Fellowship Matters Bangladesh’s climate reporting has traditionally focused on rural communities and coastal vulnerabilities. However, climate change is also reshaping the private sector , from agriculture to garments to pharmaceuticals. The uploaded document states: “A 2025 World Bank study found that 25 million workdays were lost in 2024 due to heat‑related physical and mental health issues.” “Industries in Bangladesh cannot afford to ignore their two‑way relationships with climate change.” Key issues include: Heat stress reducing productivity in factories Erratic weather damaging agricultural output Environmental degradation caused by industries Upcoming EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) impacting textile exports Climate‑driven disruptions affecting farmers and fishers Growing need for climate‑responsible business practices EJN’s fellowship supports journalists who can illuminate these complex relationships and highlight solutions emerging across Bangladesh. Fellowship Themes Applicants may pitch stories related to: Impacts of climate change on Bangladesh’s private sector and how businesses are responding Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) rules and their influence Climate finance instruments , including carbon markets New business opportunities such as renewable energy and clean mobility Artificial intelligence and its climate implications Corporate greenwashing and accountability Proposals with fresh, underreported angles are strongly preferred. Eligibility Requirements According to the uploaded document: “Applicants can be from Bangladesh only.” “Applications are open to journalists working in any medium… We welcome applications from early‑career journalists and experienced reporters.” Eligibility includes: Must be based in Bangladesh Open to individual journalists or groups (with one lead applicant) Open to print, online, TV, radio , and other reporting professionals Open to freelancers and staff journalists Applications accepted in English or Bangla Applicants must disclose any use of generative AI Applicants must submit two samples of work Applicants must include a letter of support from an editor committing to publish by September 30, 2026 Fellowship Logistics Up to 10 fellowships will be awarded Fellows will receive funding + mentorship Reports must be published by September 2026 Reports may be produced in English or Bangla (with English translation if needed) Applicants must include a risk management plan for safety Applicants may include security‑related costs in the budget Budget Requirements The uploaded document states: “We ask that the budgets be reasonable with a ceiling of 1500 EUR.” “Applications with smaller budgets will be more competitive.” Budget guidelines: Maximum budget: €1500 Must include a detailed Excel budget with justification Should focus on reporting costs (travel, accommodation, translation, safety) Should not focus on buying new equipment Freelancers may include a stipend , but it must be no more than 30% of the total budget Applicants must disclose other funding sources , if any Judging Criteria Applications will be evaluated on: Relevance to climate change and the private sector Originality and fresh angles Reach of the publishing outlet Impact potential Innovative storytelling , including multimedia and data visualization Feasibility and timely publication Strength of the editor’s support letter Application Process To apply: Click Apply Now on the EJN website Log in or create an account Complete the application form Upload: Detailed budget (Excel) Two work samples Signed editor support letter Submit before the deadline Important Notes Reports must include the tagline: “This story was produced with support from Internews’ Earth Journalism Network.” EJN and partners retain rights to edit, publish, broadcast, and distribute the stories Late applications will not be considered Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post Bangladesh Climate Private Sector Reporting Fellowships 2026: A Complete Guide for Journalists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Women 4 Water Swarovski Foundation Scholarships 2026 for Female Water Professionals to Study Master of Science in Water and Sustainable Development at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education
summary: The Women 4 Water Swarovski Foundation Scholarships 2026 offer an outstanding opportunity for female water professionals who want to advance their education, strengthen their leadership skills, and contribute to sustainable water solutions in their home countries. The scholarships are offered through the Swarovski Foundation’s Women 4 Water initiative in partnership with IHE Delft Institute for Water Education. The programme supports women who have already secured academic admission to the regular one-year Master of Science programme in Water and Sustainable Development beginning in November 2026. Through this scholarship opportunity, three full scholarships will be awarded to selected female candidates. The focus is not only on academic achievement, but also on building gender equity and leadership in the water sector. The scholarship is designed for women who are already connected to water-related work and who are ready to apply advanced knowledge in areas such as water resources, biodiversity, ecosystems, innovation, environmental modelling, climate adaptation, and sustainable development. The Women 4 Water initiative recognizes that women play a vital role in solving water challenges, but they are often underrepresented in technical, scientific, and leadership positions within the sector. By supporting female professionals from low-income and middle-income countries, the scholarship helps create stronger local capacity and promotes long-term change in communities where sustainable water management is urgently needed. About the scholarship The Swarovski Foundation, through the Women 4 Water initiative, will provide three full scholarships for female applicants who have been academically admitted to the regular one-year Master of Science programme in Water and Sustainable Development at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education. The programme begins in November 2026. The scholarship is connected to the Water Resources and Ecosystem Health track. Applicants must have selected this track as their preferred track when applying for academic admission to the programme. In addition, applicants must have selected either the Environment profile or the Digital Innovation and Hydroinformatics profile in their academic application. This makes the scholarship especially suitable for candidates whose work combines water resources, ecosystem protection, data, technology, environmental planning, biodiversity, and sustainable development. Scholarship benefits Selected candidates will receive a full scholarship to support their study in the regular one-year Master of Science programme in Water and Sustainable Development. The opportunity allows recipients to study at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education, an institution known for water education, research, and capacity development. The scholarship provides the following major benefits: Full scholarship support for selected female candidates. Access to a one-year international Master of Science programme in Water and Sustainable Development. Advanced training in water resources, ecosystem health, biodiversity, innovation, and sustainability. Opportunity to strengthen technical competencies in areas such as data analysis, modelling, planning, GIS, remote sensing, artificial intelligence, and environmental modelling. A pathway to contribute to gender equity and leadership in the water sector. Preparation to return home and apply new knowledge to local and regional water challenges. Support for women who want to work on urgent environmental issues such as wetlands, aquatic ecosystems, river basin ecology, ecosystem restoration, climate adaptation, and biodiversity monitoring. Eligible academic programme The scholarship is linked to the regular one-year Master of Science programme in Water and Sustainable Development at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education. Applicants must receive unconditional academic admission to this programme by 12 July 2026 to be considered in the initial selection round. Applications for academic admission submitted after the official deadline of 1 July 2026 will not be considered. The programme starts in November 2026 and is designed for students and professionals interested in solving complex water and sustainability problems. For this scholarship, applicants must specifically choose the Water Resources and Ecosystem Health track. Required study track and preferred profiles To be considered for the Women 4 Water Swarovski Foundation Scholarship, applicants must meet specific academic application requirements. These requirements are important because the scholarship is targeted toward candidates whose interests align with ecosystem health, biodiversity, technical innovation, and sustainable water management. Applicants must have selected: Water Resources and Ecosystem Health as their preferred track. Environment as one preferred profile, or Digital Innovation and Hydroinformatics as one preferred profile. This means the scholarship is not open to every track or profile within the programme. The applicant’s academic application must already show a clear connection to the focus areas of the Women 4 Water initiative. Priority areas for selection The scholarship gives priority to candidates whose interests and professional work connect technical skills with systems thinking. This means applicants should be able to show interest in both practical technical tools and wider environmental or social systems. The strongest candidates are likely to be those who can connect water science, biodiversity, innovation, and sustainable development in a clear and meaningful way. Priority will be given to applicants with interest or work experience in areas such as: Biodiversity and innovation. Geographic Information Systems. Remote sensing. Artificial intelligence. Environmental modelling. Data analysis. Water planning. Wetlands. Aquatic ecosystems. River basin ecology. Nature-based solutions. Ecosystem restoration. Biodiversity monitoring. Environmental flows. Climate adaptation. The scholarship places strong value on interdisciplinary approaches. This means applicants who can combine environmental knowledge, digital tools, water management, biodiversity protection, and sustainable development thinking may stand out during selection. Possible thesis focus areas Applicants whose interests connect with the scholarship’s priority areas may consider research topics that focus on the relationship between water, ecosystems, biodiversity, and innovation. The scholarship information highlights several possible thesis directions. Possible thesis topics may include: Nature-based solutions. Ecosystem restoration. Biodiversity monitoring. Environmental flows. Climate adaptation. Wetland protection. Aquatic ecosystem management. River basin ecology. Water and biodiversity planning. Digital innovation for water and ecosystem management. These topics show that the scholarship is strongly connected to practical environmental problem-solving. It is especially relevant for applicants who want to use science, technology, and policy knowledge to improve water systems and protect ecosystems. Application procedure Applicants do not need to email IHE Delft to request consideration for the scholarship. The selection process is handled through the official procedure. Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria and who have received unconditional academic admission to the regular one-year Master of Science programme in Water and Sustainable Development by 12 July 2026 will be considered in the initial selection round. The application process follows these stages: Apply for academic admission to the regular one-year Master of Science programme in Water and Sustainable Development. Select the Water Resources and Ecosystem Health track as the preferred track. Select either Environment or Digital Innovation and Hydroinformatics as the preferred profile. Ensure the academic admission application is submitted before the official deadline of 1 July 2026. Receive unconditional academic admission by 12 July 2026. Eligible admitted candidates will be reviewed in the initial scholarship selection round. A shortlist will be created based on candidates who best match the priority criteria. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to complete a brief scholarship application form. A further selection will be made from the submitted scholarship applications. A limited number of candidates will be invited for an interview. After the interviews, three candidates will be selected to receive the scholarship. Applicants should pay close attention to the difference between the academic admission deadline and the unconditional admission requirement. The academic application must be submitted by 1 July 2026 , but applicants must receive unconditional academic admission by 12 July 2026 to be considered for the scholarship. Important deadline The official academic admission application deadline is 1 July 2026 . Applications for academic admission received after this date will not be considered for the scholarship. Applicants must also have unconditional academic admission by 12 July 2026 to be included in the initial scholarship selection round. Important notes for applicants Applicants should carefully follow the official instructions because the scholarship process has strict conditions. Important points include: Do not send an email to IHE Delft requesting scholarship consideration. All eligible applicants will be assessed through the official selection procedure. Enquiries asking for individual scholarship consideration will not receive a response. Applicants with conditional admission are not eligible. Applicants who have already received another scholarship will not be considered. Applicants must receive unconditional academic admission by 12 July 2026. Academic admission applications submitted after 1 July 2026 will not be considered. Only three candidates will receive the scholarship award. How to apply Interested applicants should first apply for academic admission to the regular one-year Master of Science programme in Water and Sustainable Development at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education. During the academic application, applicants must select the Water Resources and Ecosystem Health track and choose either the Environment profile or the Digital Innovation and Hydroinformatics profile. Applicants who already have unconditional academic admission and meet the eligibility criteria will be considered in the initial selection round. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Women 4 Water Swarovski Foundation Scholarships 2026 for Female Water Professionals to Study Master of Science in Water and Sustainable Development at IHE Delft Institute for Water Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Digital Minds Career Development Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Remote Opportunity for Researchers, Policy Experts, AI Professionals, and Future Leaders Exploring Artificial Intelligence Sentience and Digital Minds
summary: The Digital Minds Career Development Fellowships 2026 are now open for applications, offering a unique opportunity for individuals interested in exploring some of the most important and emerging questions surrounding artificial intelligence (AI), consciousness, sentience, moral status, and digital welfare. Offered by Longview Philanthropy in collaboration with Future Impact Group, this fellowship is designed to support talented individuals who want to transition into, deepen, or expand their work in research, policy, advocacy, communications, entrepreneurship, academia, and other fields related to digital minds and AI sentience. As AI systems continue to advance rapidly, questions about whether future AI systems could possess forms of consciousness, sentience, or morally relevant experiences are becoming increasingly important. The Digital Minds Career Development Fellowships seek to cultivate a new generation of researchers, policymakers, practitioners, and innovators who can contribute meaningfully to these discussions and help shape responsible approaches to the future of AI. The fellowship provides financial support, networking opportunities, mentorship-related benefits, access to resources, and participation in leading discussions on digital minds and AI ethics. About the Digital Minds Career Development Fellowships The Digital Minds Career Development Fellowships are intended to help promising individuals dedicate significant time to exploring, developing, and advancing careers focused on digital minds issues. The program recognizes that many talented individuals may have the skills and motivation to contribute to this growing field but lack the financial support necessary to pursue career transitions, advanced studies, independent research, internships, policy work, or entrepreneurial exploration. Through this fellowship, recipients receive funding and professional support that enables them to build expertise, gain practical experience, and identify impactful long-term career pathways. The initiative is funded by Longview Philanthropy, while Future Impact Group assists with networking opportunities and application review processes. Fellowship Benefits Successful fellows will receive a range of benefits designed to support their professional growth and career exploration. Financial Support Recipients will receive: A stipend calibrated according to: Duration of the fellowship Cost of living considerations Previous professional experience Additional funding for: Research activities Travel and logistics Project-related expenses Other approved needs on a case-by-case basis Professional Development Opportunities Selected fellows will gain access to: Valuable networking opportunities with experts and organizations working in the digital minds field Connections with researchers, policymakers, academics, and practitioners Career development support Exposure to leading discussions on AI sentience and ethics Access to Resources Where applicable, fellows may receive: Access to computational resources Research support opportunities Connections to advisors and mentors Mind, Ethics, and Policy Summit Invitation A particularly valuable benefit of the fellowship is an invitation to attend the annual Mind, Ethics, and Policy Summit at New York University (NYU), where participants can engage with leading thinkers working on AI ethics, consciousness research, and governance challenges. Who the Fellowship Is Looking For The Digital Minds Career Development Fellowships welcome applicants from a wide variety of academic and professional backgrounds. Examples of individuals who may be strong candidates include: Graduate Students Individuals currently pursuing or planning to pursue graduate-level studies related to digital minds, AI ethics, consciousness studies, philosophy of mind, AI governance, or related disciplines. Technical Artificial Intelligence Researchers Researchers who wish to: Explore digital minds research topics Attend conferences and events Connect with organizations in the field Identify high-impact career opportunities Communications Professionals Professionals interested in transitioning into digital minds work through activities such as: Conducting surveys Building expert platforms Public engagement Strategic communications Non-Profit Entrepreneurs Aspiring founders who wish to spend dedicated time: Exploring opportunities in the digital minds ecosystem Identifying gaps in the field Designing impactful organizations Preparing future non-profit initiatives Government and Policy Professionals Including: Civil servants Diplomats Legal researchers Policy analysts These professionals may use the fellowship to investigate governance approaches and policy solutions related to digital minds and AI welfare. Participants of Artificial Intelligence Safety Fellowship Programs Individuals involved in AI safety initiatives such as: Machine Learning Alignment and Theory Scholars (MATS) Future Impact Group programs Similar mentorship-based fellowships These applicants may continue working with mentors while developing expertise in digital minds issues. Importantly, applicants do not need to fit neatly into any of the above categories. Individuals with strong potential and compelling plans are encouraged to apply. Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on three major factors. 1. Demonstrated Interest in Digital Sentience Applicants should show evidence that they have seriously engaged with questions related to: AI consciousness Digital sentience Moral status of AI systems AI welfare AI governance Related ethical and policy challenges Strong applicants will demonstrate an understanding of how these topics connect to broader AI risk discussions. 2. Potential for High-Impact Contributions Evaluators will consider evidence such as: Academic achievements Employment history Research accomplishments Leadership experience Professional achievements References and recommendations 3. Quality of Fellowship Plans Applicants should present thoughtful plans outlining: Proposed fellowship activities Learning objectives Research goals Career development pathways Future impact potential Applications that include mentorship arrangements or advisory support may be particularly competitive. Funding Priority Considerations Longview Philanthropy places special emphasis on supporting applicants who: Would struggle to pursue this work without fellowship funding Have limited access to alternative funding sources Face restrictions under existing funding arrangements Eligible Fellowship Activities The fellowship supports a broad range of activities aimed at building career capital and expertise. Examples include: Academic and Research Activities Graduate study Postdoctoral research Independent research projects Academic course buy-outs Professional Experience Opportunities Unpaid internships Low-paid internships Research assistantships Policy placements Career Development Activities Independent study Technical skill development Policy training Career exploration periods Career transition support Entrepreneurship and Innovation Exploring new organizational ideas Non-profit incubation activities Organizational design research Transition Support Bridging stipends between positions Transitional funding during career shifts Support for moving into digital minds-focused work Application Requirements Applicants must submit several components as part of their application package. These include: Biographical and logistical information A resume, curriculum vitae (CV), or LinkedIn profile covering: Employment history Educational background Major achievements A statement discussing motivation and interest in digital minds topics (approximately 300 words) A detailed description of planned fellowship activities (approximately 200–1000 words) A discussion of potential post-fellowship plans (approximately 300 words) Information about alternative funding options or plans if fellowship funding is unavailable Funding request details and proposed timeline A short explanation of what the applicant would do if they do not receive fellowship funding (approximately 150 words) How to Apply Interested candidates should complete the official application form and submit all required materials before the deadline. Applicants are encouraged to prepare thoughtful responses that clearly demonstrate: Genuine engagement with digital minds issues Strong potential for impactful work Realistic and ambitious fellowship plans Long-term commitment to contributing to the field Early preparation is recommended to ensure sufficient time for gathering supporting materials and refining application essays. Application Deadline Deadline: July 10, 2026 Candidates should submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for this fellowship cycle. Contact Information For questions regarding the application process, applicants may contact: Email: digitalminds-rfp@longview.org APPLY HERE Final Thoughts The Digital Minds Career Development Fellowships represent an exceptional opportunity for individuals who want to contribute to one of the most intellectually challenging and socially significant areas emerging from advances in artificial intelligence. Whether you are a researcher, student, policy professional, entrepreneur, communicator, or AI practitioner, this fellowship provides the funding, resources, community, and flexibility needed to explore impactful work at the intersection of AI, consciousness, ethics, governance, and digital welfare. For individuals seeking to build a meaningful career addressing the future implications of digital minds, this fellowship could serve as an important stepping stone toward long-term impact and leadership in the field. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Digital Minds Career Development Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Remote Opportunity for Researchers, Policy Experts, AI Professionals, and Future Leaders Exploring Artificial Intelligence Sentience and Digital Minds appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Marshall Scholarship: A Complete, In‑Depth Guide for U.S. Applicants
summary: The Marshall Scholarship remains one of the most prestigious and competitive academic awards available to U.S. students seeking to pursue graduate study in the United Kingdom. With applications for the 2027 Marshall Scholarship now officially open , prospective candidates must understand the full application process, eligibility rules, deadlines, and expectations set by the Marshall Aid Commemoration Commission. This comprehensive guide expands every detail to help applicants prepare a strong, well‑structured, and timely submission. Overview of the Marshall Scholarship Application Process The Marshall Scholarship supports intellectually distinguished young Americans to study at any UK university. The program seeks individuals who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, and a commitment to strengthening the U.S.–UK relationship. The application process is rigorous, multi‑layered, and requires early preparation. Applicants must complete an online application, secure institutional endorsement, and submit all required materials—including recommendations—by strict deadlines. The selection process is conducted across eight U.S. regional committees, each responsible for reviewing and interviewing shortlisted candidates. Before You Apply: Essential Preparation Steps Prospective applicants should begin by thoroughly reviewing all official guidance provided by the Marshall Commission. This includes: 1. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must ensure they meet all academic, citizenship, and residency criteria. The eligibility rules outline who may apply, what academic standing is required, and how regional application boundaries are determined. 2. Scholarship Pathways The Marshall Scholarship offers different pathways, including one‑year and two‑year options. Candidates must choose only one pathway. Applying to both results in automatic disqualification. 3. Rules for Candidates The Rules for Candidates outline the expectations, restrictions, and obligations associated with applying. Applicants should pay close attention to the program’s objectives, as these values guide the selection committees. 4. Evaluation Criteria Selection committees evaluate candidates based on: Academic merit Leadership potential Ambassadorial potential and commitment to U.S.–UK relations Understanding these criteria helps applicants craft stronger essays and present a cohesive narrative. 5. Marshall Partnership Scholarships Applicants should review partnership opportunities that may offer additional benefits or specific academic pathways. Rules and Guidance Documents Applicants are strongly encouraged to read the following official documents: 2027 Rules for Marshall Scholarship Candidates 2027 Statements for Marshall Scholarship Application ASAMS Application System Guidance (PDF) ASAMS Application System Guidance (Video) These resources provide detailed instructions for completing the online application, writing required statements, and navigating the submission platform. How to Submit an Application The Marshall Scholarship application process involves both the applicant and their undergraduate institution. The steps include: 1. Complete the Online Application Form Candidates must fill out the official online application form provided through the ASAMS system. All essays, personal statements, and required information must be entered directly into the platform. 2. Submit to Undergraduate Institution Once completed, the application must be submitted through the system to the applicant’s U.S. undergraduate institution. The institution reviews the application for completeness and decides whether to endorse the candidate. 3. Institutional Endorsement If the institution chooses to endorse the application, it will: Add an official letter of endorsement Submit the full application to the appropriate regional committee Applicants should contact their institutional advisor early, as internal deadlines may be significantly earlier than the official Marshall deadlines. 4. Recommendation Letters Three letters of recommendation must be submitted by the recommenders through the online system by the applicant deadline. 5. Application Caps Each endorsing institution may submit no more than 24 applications overall. However, there is no limit on how many applications may be submitted to a single region. Important Deadlines for the 2027 Marshall Scholarship Competition All deadlines occur at 5:00 PM in the time zone of the endorsing institution . 1. Applicant Recommender Deadline 29 September 2026 All applicants and recommenders must submit their materials by this date. No changes can be made after submission. 2. Institutional Endorsement Deadline 1 October 2026 The endorsing institution must submit the final application—including the endorsement letter and all recommendations—by this date. Applicants can track their application status online throughout the process. What Happens After Submission 1. Regional Review Applications are reviewed by one of eight regional centers across the United States. 2. Shortlisting Shortlisted candidates are notified in early to mid‑November and invited to interview. Interview dates and guidance are provided on the Marshall website. 3. Final Selection Final decisions are made shortly after interviews. Selected candidates receive written notification and must accept or decline the award within five days . 4. University Admission Requirement All awards are contingent upon the candidate receiving an offer from a UK university. Accessing the Application Applicants may begin the process using the official link Note: Undergraduate advisors must register in the system, and processing may take 2–3 business days. Deadline: 29 September 2026 (Applicants Recommenders) 1 October 2026 (Institutional Endorsement) Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more scholarship opportunities here The post Marshall Scholarship: A Complete, In‑Depth Guide for U.S. Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fall 2026 Young Advocates Program in Houston, Texas – A Transformative Leadership & Advocacy Training Experience for Young Adults
summary: The Fall 2026 Young Advocates Program by Young Invincibles (YI) is a powerful, hands‑on leadership development initiative designed to equip young adults with the skills, confidence, and real‑world experience needed to drive meaningful change in their communities. As stated in the program description, “This unique, hands-on program trains young people to engage in their communities around the major issues shaping our generation.” This program is ideal for individuals passionate about higher education , healthcare access , workforce development , and civic engagement , and who want to become part of a growing movement of young leaders shaping policy and amplifying youth voices nationwide. About Young Invincibles (YI) Young Invincibles is a nonprofit, nonpartisan organization committed to empowering young adults and expanding economic opportunity. According to the document, YI’s mission is to “amplify the voices of young adults in the political process and expand economic opportunity for our generation.” The organization operates regional offices in California , Colorado , and Texas , and works to strengthen youth voices across major issue areas including: Higher education affordability Healthcare access Workforce and job quality Civic engagement and voter participation Program Overview The Young Advocates Program is open to individuals ages 18–34 who live in or attend school in California, Colorado, or Texas . The program provides a structured, cohort‑based experience where participants collaborate weekly and engage in hands‑on advocacy projects. Key Activities Include: Organizing Campaigns Focused on higher education, healthcare, worker justice, and civic engagement. Policy Development Helping shape policy priorities that impact young adults. Event Support Leadership Supporting statewide conferences, community events, and outreach activities. Voter Engagement Leading efforts to mobilize young voters. Healthcare Enrollment Promotion Supporting campaigns that increase access to healthcare. Digital Content Creation Producing advocacy‑focused content for online platforms. Recruitment Mobilization Encouraging other young adults to participate in community action. Time Commitment Structure The program emphasizes consistency and collaboration. As stated in the document, “This program is designed as a cohort experience where everyone’s participation matters.” Program Dates: September 18, 2026 – December 11, 2026 (12‑week program) Weekly Session Times: California: Fridays, 10:00 am – 2:00 pm PT (virtual) Colorado: Fridays, 9:00 am – 1:00 pm MT (virtual) Texas: Tuesdays, 4:00 – 6:30 pm CT (virtual) Fridays, 12:00 – 2:30 pm CT (virtual) In‑person meetings on the last Friday of each month Additional Expectations: Attend Orientation on September 18, 2026 (in person) Attend Program Close on December 11, 2026 (in person) Participate in in‑person events such as: Policy Shark Tank State of Young California Community outreach events Attend weekly sessions with cameras on Attend two in‑person meetings per month Complete assignments between sessions Compensation Advocates are paid hourly for training and project work, averaging 6–10 hours per week . Hourly Pay Rates by State: California: $18.42 Colorado: $19.29 Texas: $15.00 Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria: Must be 18 or older Must be eligible to work in the United States Must commit to the entire program duration Must align with YI’s mission, vision, and values Must demonstrate interest in higher education, healthcare, or workforce issues Must be able to work independently and collaboratively Must show professionalism, maturity, and initiative Must be creative, flexible, and able to thrive in a fast‑paced environment Must have access to a laptop and WiFi for virtual sessions Application Process Deadline Applicants must be available for all required sessions and events. The application includes: Resume Cover letter References Responses to short‑answer questions Confirmation of age and student status Application Deadline: July 17, 2026 at midnight Eastern Time For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post Fall 2026 Young Advocates Program in Houston, Texas – A Transformative Leadership Advocacy Training Experience for Young Adults appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ITTO Fellowship Programme 2026 — Empowering the Next Generation of Tropical Forestry Leaders
summary: The International Tropical Timber Organization (ITTO) continues its long-standing commitment to strengthening global forestry expertise through the Freezailah Fellowship Fund . The ITTO Fellowship Programme 2026 is now officially open, offering a powerful opportunity for young and mid‑career professionals across ITTO member countries to advance their skills, contribute to sustainable forest management, and elevate their careers. Overview of the ITTO Fellowship Programme Since its establishment in 1989 , the ITTO Fellowship Programme has played a transformative role in building human capacity in tropical forestry and related disciplines. The programme supports short-term professional development activities that directly contribute to: Sustainable management of tropical forests Efficient use and processing of tropical timber Improved economic information on international tropical timber trade The fellowship is funded through the Freezailah Fellowship Fund , supported by generous contributions from multiple countries and partners. Programme Achievements and Global Impact 📌 Reach and Participation As of December 2021 , the programme has supported: 1400+ fellows From 49+ countries Working in government, academia, research institutions, civil society, and the private sector 📌 Regional Distribution of Fellowships Africa: 32% Asia-Pacific: 30.6% Latin America Caribbean: 24.9% Developing consumer countries: 9.6% Developed consumer countries: 3.1% (activities conducted in producer member countries) 📌 Thematic Areas Supported Reforestation Forest Management: 71.7% Forest Industry: 19.8% Economics, Statistics Markets: 8.5% 📌 Financial Contributions Total funding to date: US$9.8 million , contributed by: Japan (68%) USA (19%) Netherlands (7%) Australia (2.5%) Others (3.5%) including Sweden, UK, Bali Partnership Fund-B, and private companies 📌 Support for Women Foresters The programme strongly encourages women’s participation: 424 women fellows supported Representing 30%+ of all fellowships awarded Impact Assessment Findings Two major impact assessments (2010 and 2016) highlight the programme’s significant contributions: Professional and Institutional Impact 82% contributed moderately or significantly to reforestation/forest conservation in their home countries 88% improved productivity and performance in their institutions 42% influenced national forestry or environmental policy 61% now work on international forestry issues such as climate change and REDD Academic and Career Advancement 85% hold advanced degrees (PhD or Master’s) Over one-third earned these degrees with fellowship support 60%+ received job promotions or new positions immediately after their fellowship 73% reported that their career advancement was directly related to skills gained through the programme ITTO Fellowship Programme 2026 — Application Details The 2026 cycle is now open for applications. 📅 Key Dates Application Deadline: 20 July 2026 (Monday), 17:00 Japan Standard Time (JST) Earliest Start Date for Fellowship Activities: After 1 January 2027 Results Announcement: Around 25 November 2026 , following the 62nd ITTC 💰 Fellowship Grant Amount Maximum grant: US$10,000 📌 Eligible Activities The programme primarily supports short-term professional development , including: Participation in international conferences Training courses Study tours Preparation of manuals or monographs Small grants for postgraduate study 📌 Eligibility Open only to nationals of ITTO member countries Priority given to nationals of developing member countries Applicants can access the Information Note 2026 and the estimated selection timeline via the ITTO website. Why This Fellowship Matters The ITTO Fellowship Programme is more than a funding opportunity—it is a catalyst for: Strengthening global forestry leadership Enhancing sustainable forest management Supporting climate action and biodiversity conservation Empowering professionals to influence policy and institutional change With a proven track record of transforming careers and institutions, the 2026 cycle offers another chance for emerging forestry leaders to make a lasting impact. Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities here The post ITTO Fellowship Programme 2026 — Empowering the Next Generation of Tropical Forestry Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Research Associate – Foreign Policy and Security Studies at CSEP (The Africa Research Alliance), New Delhi, India
summary: Exciting Research Career Opportunity in Foreign Policy and Security Studies Professionals and emerging researchers interested in international relations, foreign policy, security studies, and Africa-focused research now have an excellent opportunity to join one of India’s leading public policy think tanks. The Centre for Social and Economic Progress (CSEP) is currently hiring a Research Associate for The Africa Research Alliance (TARA), a development-oriented research hub operating within its Foreign Policy and Security Studies programme. This position offers a unique chance to contribute to policy-relevant research, engage with experts and policymakers, and support initiatives that strengthen understanding of Africa’s development, governance, security, and international relations. The role is based in New Delhi, India, and is a full-time position. About the Centre for Social and Economic Progress (CSEP) The Centre for Social and Economic Progress (CSEP) is an independent public policy think tank dedicated to conducting rigorous research and analysis on critical issues affecting India and the world. The organization is known for producing evidence-based policy recommendations and fostering dialogue among policymakers, researchers, academics, and development practitioners. CSEP s research spans several thematic areas, including: Economic growth and development Finance and development Energy and sustainability Foreign policy and security Governance and public policy International cooperation and diplomacy The institution has established itself as a respected voice in public policy research and continues to influence policy discussions through high-quality research and stakeholder engagement. About The Africa Research Alliance (TARA) The Africa Research Alliance (TARA) serves as a dedicated research platform within CSEP’s Foreign Policy and Security Studies vertical. The initiative focuses on Africa-related development and policy issues while promoting deeper understanding and engagement between India and African countries. As geopolitical, economic, and security relationships between Africa and the rest of the world continue to evolve, TARA plays an important role in generating research that supports informed policy decisions and meaningful partnerships. Role and Responsibilities The selected Research Associate will play an important role in supporting the alliance’s research activities and policy engagement efforts. Key responsibilities are expected to include: Conducting policy and academic research on foreign policy, security, and development issues. Monitoring and analyzing emerging policy developments and geopolitical trends. Supporting the preparation of research papers, policy briefs, reports, and analytical publications. Assisting in stakeholder engagement activities and policy dialogues. Contributing to the organization of workshops, conferences, seminars, and research events. Supporting collaborative research initiatives involving experts, policymakers, and development practitioners. The role provides an excellent opportunity for individuals seeking practical experience in policy research and international affairs. Why You Should Apply Working at CSEP offers several professional advantages, including: 1. Exposure to High-Level Policy Research Employees contribute to research that addresses pressing regional and global challenges and informs public policy discussions. 2. Work with Leading Experts Researchers collaborate with experienced scholars, policy specialists, and international experts across multiple disciplines. 3. Build a Strong Professional Network The organization regularly engages with government officials, diplomats, academics, development organizations, and industry stakeholders. 4. Gain Africa-Focused Research Experience The position provides valuable exposure to African development, diplomacy, governance, and security issues, making it especially attractive for candidates interested in Africa-related policy research. 5. Enhance Research and Analytical Skills The role requires rigorous research, policy analysis, writing, and stakeholder engagement, helping candidates strengthen skills that are highly valued across academia, government, think tanks, and international organizations. Location New Delhi, India Full-Time Position Application Information Interested candidates should review the complete vacancy announcement and application instructions through the official CSEP careers page . Deadline The job announcement currently available does not publicly specify a closing date. Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible before the position is filled. Final Thoughts The Research Associate – Foreign Policy and Security Studies position at CSEP represents an outstanding opportunity for professionals seeking to build a career in policy research, international affairs, security studies, and Africa-focused development research. Through The Africa Research Alliance, successful candidates will have the chance to contribute to impactful research, engage with influential stakeholders, and help shape important policy conversations concerning Africa, India, and the broader international community. For aspiring policy researchers, international relations graduates, and development professionals, this position offers a strong platform for career growth and meaningful impact in the world of public policy research. Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here The post Research Associate – Foreign Policy and Security Studies at CSEP (The Africa Research Alliance), New Delhi, India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
